title: erigeron divaricatus
intro: Erigeron divaricatus, also known as the variable fleabane, is a perennial herb in the daisy family that grows in dry, rocky areas and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The health benefits of Erigeron divaricatus include reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds and skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
